mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.09% as of October 2013) compresses the equity risk premium discount rate, mechanically supporting elevated equity valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX drifts negative in more than 45% of low-rate periods since 2009.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that raises expected corporate earnings and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE declined from 7.3% (Oct vintage) to revised 7.2%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show negative or flat forward 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases across the 2010-2013 recovery sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized consumer and residential investment strength that is not captured by either series alone; only when both indicators are rising together does aggregate demand momentum become strong enough to lift forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 AND HOUST 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 917.0 thousand units in August 2013 vintage
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce forward 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series models outperform the joint interaction model out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of the prevailing macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.082 (revised) October 2013 vintage, up from prior months
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing growth rate and forward 60-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) regime combined with low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 2%), the Federal Reserve has policy room to maintain accommodative conditions, sustaining a positive equity risk premium; this positive relationship reverses once inflation accelerates enough to threaten early tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 233.544 for Sept 2013, implying sub-2% YoY inflation
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) period with low inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns turn negative in more than 40% of ZIRP-and-low-inflation periods, or the positive relationship fails to reverse when CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5% in later out-of-sample data.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) accelerates and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously declines, the combination confirms genuine labor market tightening rather than a statistical artifact of labor force exits, generating a stronger positive equity signal over the next month than either series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150k AND UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 137041 thousand for Oct 2013, UNRATE revised to 7.2%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not outperform single-series PAYEMS-only or UNRATE-only signals in walk-forward Rank IC comparison, or produce forward returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing broader demand deceleration that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as markets price in slower growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 850 thousand units (annualized) or drops more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 917.0 thousand units August 2013, showing volatility around this range
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST-decline instances show positive or flat forward 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a Federal Reserve balance-sheet tapering regime, a deceleration in industrial production growth signals that reduced monetary accommodation is coinciding with weakening real output, amplifying downside risk to equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets reassess growth-liquidity tradeoffs.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth turns negative while Fed is signaling asset-purchase tapering
evidence_refs: fact:market anticipating Fed taper decision as of Nov 2013 amid low FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration during tapering signals are positive in more than 55% of instances, or effect disappears outside tapering-adjacent windows.
